objective.subject: English
objective.statement: Use capital letters and sentence-ending punctuation accurately.
misconceptions: Uses a capital letter at the start but forgets the end mark.
Uses full stops for every sentence, including questions.
Adds punctuation after every word rather than at the sentence boundary.
objective.parent_explanation: Can build simple sentences with capital letters and the right end punctuation.
objective.teacher_evidence: Uses capital letters, full stops and question marks with improving sentence-boundary control.

activities.title: I can build a sentence that starts and ends in the right way.
activities.prompt: The sentence path needs a start gate and an end sign.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate the idea that a sentence has a clear beginning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Look for the word that opens the sentence gate.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A story path begins at a glowing capital-letter gate.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The sentence path needs a start gate and an end sign.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce sentence punctuation as a boundary marker, not decoration.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A capital letter opens the sentence. A punctuation mark closes it.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Words travel along a path from capital gate to punctuation sign.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Where is my red hat? starts with a capital letter and ends with a question mark.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model choosing punctuation from sentence meaning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: This sentence is asking something, so it needs a question mark.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A question bubble connects to the end mark.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Drag the capital letter, words and end mark into the sentence path.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise sentence construction with explicit boundary cues.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Build one complete idea from start to end.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Word tiles snap into a path with fixed start and end slots.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Is this telling, asking or shouting?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Diagnose whether the learner chooses punctuation by meaning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Listen to what the sentence is doing.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three end signs pulse: full stop, question mark, exclamation mark.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Show Pip why this sentence needs a question mark.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation to confirm sentence-function understanding.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Can you teach me which end sign I need?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion offers a wrong end sign and waits for correction.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A group of words that makes one complete idea.
vocabulary.audio_script: A sentence tells one complete idea.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A story block with a start gate and end gate.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A mark that shows someone is asking something.
vocabulary.audio_script: A question mark shows the sentence asks something.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A talking character holding a question bubble.

manipulative.purpose: Let children build a complete sentence from capital letter to punctuation mark.
manipulative.interaction_notes: Learners drag word tiles and punctuation signs into start, middle and end slots.
manipulative.accessibility_notes: Tap-to-place mode, audio replay and reduced-motion tile highlighting are available.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use static story-path highlights and quiet confirmation.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace tile snapping with instant placement and outline feedback.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read every sentence aloud before asking for punctuation.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use short familiar sentences, chunked tiles and optional picture anchors.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Show one sentence and one goal at a time.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Frame editing as helping the story path close properly.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach asking words and provide picture-supported vocabulary.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build simple telling sentences with a capital letter and full stop.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: familiar noun phrase + verb phrase + start/end boundary slots
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check reading load, vocabulary familiarity and sentence completeness.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Distinguish asking sentences from telling sentences.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: spoken sentence + three end-mark choices + meaning cue
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Audio should make question intonation clear without giving away the answer visually.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Edit sentence boundaries without scattering punctuation through the sentence.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: uncapitalised or unpunctuated sentence + edit targets
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep edits short enough for Year 2 working memory.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Provide spaced review in short story contexts.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two-sentence story extract + one punctuation repair
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid assessing comprehension and punctuation at the same time in early retrieval.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Offer equivalent practice with less movement and fewer visual effects.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: same sentence functions with static highlight feedback
variant_blueprints.review_notes: SEND review should check reduced visual clutter and predictable response states.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Uses a capital letter at the start but forgets the end mark.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner selects the capital start but leaves the sentence open.
repair_sequence: Show the sentence path with an open end gate.
Ask the learner to choose the sign that closes the idea.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner completes a new sentence with both start and end boundary markers.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Uses full stops for every sentence, including questions.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses a full stop after a who, what, where, why or how prompt.
repair_sequence: Replay the sentence with question intonation.
Match the asking sentence to a question mark.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner correctly punctuates a new asking sentence.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Adds punctuation after every word rather than at the sentence boundary.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner places end marks between words.
repair_sequence: Group the words into one complete idea.
Show that the punctuation sign belongs at the end gate.
Rebuild the sentence as one path.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner edits a sentence with only one correct end mark.

feedback.companion_prompt: Show Pip why this sentence needs a question mark.
feedback.parent_summary: Can build simple sentences with capital letters and the right end punctuation.
feedback.teacher_evidence: Uses capital letters, full stops and question marks with improving sentence-boundary control.
next_actions: Add exclamation sentences once full stop/question mark choice is stable.
Move to two-sentence editing with clear boundaries.

body.prompt: Build the sentence: the dog runs.

hints: This sentence is telling, so it ends with a full stop.
questions.explanation: The sentence starts with The and ends with a full stop because it is telling something.

body.prompt: Choose the end mark: Where is my red hat

hints: Questions end with a question mark.
questions.explanation: Where is my red hat? is a question, so it needs a question mark.

body.prompt: Fix the sentence: my cat is sleeping

hints: The first word needs a capital letter.
questions.explanation: My cat is sleeping. starts with a capital letter and ends with a full stop.

reward_payload.explanation: Can build simple sentences with capital letters and the right end punctuation.
reward_payload.companion_prompt: Show Pip why this sentence needs a question mark.
